authoritative_sources.title: 29 CFR Part 1926 - Safety and Health Regulations for Construction
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The current eCFR text is the primary federal construction safety baseline, making it authoritative for when hazards, stop-work conditions, or changed site conditions invalidate analytics outputs.
used_for: refusal triggers when unsafe conditions materially change the answer

authoritative_sources.title: Work Permits Guide
authoritative_sources.publisher: New York City Department of Buildings
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public permitting, inspection, or field-service guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: This official DOB guide is authoritative for showing how permits, approved plans, permit holders, and licensed design-professional roles structure the legal start-work boundary for commercial construction.
used_for: licensed design and permit-holder boundary handling

authoritative_sources.title: Project Requirements: Project Sign-Off and Completion
authoritative_sources.publisher: New York City Department of Buildings
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public permitting, inspection, or field-service guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: This official AHJ sign-off guidance is authoritative for required inspections, objection resolution, as-built submission, and final signoff gates that directly affect completion metrics and closeout posture.

authoritative_sources.title: P-120, Public Buildings Service Cost and Schedule Management Policy
authoritative_sources.source_class: Scheduling, subcontractor, or work-order operating standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GSA's public cost and schedule policy is authoritative for baseline schedule control, progress measurement discipline, and scope-cost-schedule interfaces used in commercial construction analytics.

authoritative_sources.title: Use our project schedule management for small projects
authoritative_sources.source_class: Scheduling, subcontractor, or work-order operating standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GSA's public schedule-management workflow guidance is authoritative for update cadence, schedule-issue categories, and communication discipline relevant to progress analytics and trend notes.
used_for: scenario design for stale or conflicting progress signals

authoritative_sources.title: Construction Quality Management for Contractors
authoritative_sources.source_class: Scheduling, subcontractor, or work-order operating standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: USACE construction quality management guidance is an authoritative public operating reference for contractor QC duties, field evidence, inspection phases, and corrective-action workflow used in construction analytics.
used_for: field evidence taxonomy for normal in-scope analysis

authoritative_sources.title: Commissioning Documents: Process, Contents, and Acceptance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: WBDG is a recognized federal and industry public building guidance platform, and this reference is authoritative for issues logs, testing records, systems manuals, and turnover documentation used in closeout analytics.

authoritative_sources.title: Management's Discussion and Analysis, Selected Financial Data, and Supplementary Financial Information
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: SEC KPI and MD&A guidance is the authoritative public baseline for defining, contextualizing, and caveating metrics when commercial construction analytics are used in external or investor-style disclosure contexts.
used_for: confidence and caveat requirements for audience-facing outputs

workflow_stages.name: Source collection and qualification
workflow_stages.description: Collect work-order, permit, inspection, daily-log, schedule, change-order, safety, subcontractor, punch, and closeout records and determine whether the evidence set is current and sufficient for analytics.
source_classes: Public permitting, inspection, or field-service guidance
Scheduling, subcontractor, or work-order operating standards

workflow_stages.name: Metric definition and source hierarchy
workflow_stages.description: Resolve metric logic, denominator rules, date windows, benchmark assumptions, and system precedence before analytical or narrative claims are assembled.
source_classes: Scheduling, subcontractor, or work-order operating standards

workflow_stages.name: Analytical package assembly
workflow_stages.description: Build trend notes, variance summaries, readiness indicators, and exception packages while preserving trade, permit, inspection, subcontractor, and closeout context.
source_classes: Scheduling, subcontractor, or work-order operating standards
Public permitting, inspection, or field-service guidance

workflow_stages.name: Narrative synthesis and disclosure-risk review
workflow_stages.description: Check that no unsupported compliance, completion, benchmark, causal, or signoff language appears and that confidence posture matches the evidence set.
source_classes: Scheduling, subcontractor, or work-order operating standards

workflow_stages.name: Exception and adjacent-authority escalation
workflow_stages.description: Escalate stale, conflicting, incomplete, safety-altering, permit-sensitive, inspection-sensitive, closeout-sensitive, or licensed-authority-sensitive cases instead of synthesizing unsupported certainty.
source_classes: Public permitting, inspection, or field-service guidance
Scheduling, subcontractor, or work-order operating standards

artifacts: work orders, phase codes, cost codes, and subcontractor assignment records
permits, approved plans, permit status records, inspection requests, inspection reports, and AHJ correspondence
daily logs, superintendent notes, QC reports, field photos, safety observations, stop-work notices, and incident records
baseline schedules, look-aheads, percent-complete records, milestone trackers, delay logs, and recovery plans
change orders, RFI-derived scope clarifications, punch lists, closeout trackers, as-built status, and turnover packages
owner reporting templates, tenant metric dictionaries, benchmark definitions, and disclosure approval notes

allowed: Classify commercial construction analytics requests as in scope, out of scope, or blocked on missing prerequisites.
Define or validate metrics, source hierarchy, denominator logic, refresh assumptions, and caveat language from commercial construction records.
Prepare analytical briefs, reconciliation notes, and disclosure-risk escalations without overstating permit, code, inspection, signoff, or closeout authority.
escalate: Required tenant facts, record locations, metric definitions, benchmark methodology, or source precedence rules are missing and materially change the output.
Permit status, inspection posture, safety incidents, stop-work conditions, or closeout blockers materially change the analytical answer.
Public guidance, jurisdiction rules, or tenant policy conflict on metric eligibility, readiness, completion, benchmark, or disclosure language.
Requests cross into project controls ownership, superintendent action ownership, legal interpretation, design authority, or licensed trade signoff.
refuse: Requests to imply permit approval, code interpretation, licensed trade signoff, engineer-of-record judgment, final inspection acceptance, or owner turnover approval.
Requests to publish unsupported causal, benchmark, policy, compliance, or final-completion claims not supported by the evidence set.
Requests to approve change orders, direct subcontractor work, negotiate with inspectors, or override safety, permit, inspection, or closeout blockers.

domain_failure_modes: Publishing a percent-complete or readiness metric even though denominator logic is disputed across schedule, field, and inspection records.
Treating stale permit, inspection, subcontractor, or closeout records as current and overstating readiness or completion.
Confusing declining punch counts or temporary occupancy signals with final signoff, final acceptance, or code-compliant completion.
Turning analytics into project-controls, superintendent, legal, or licensed-trade authority instead of staying inside evidence-backed analysis.
Ignoring safety or inspection corrections that materially alter whether a trend or forecast can be published confidently.
Producing generic dashboard language that drops site, trade, permit, inspection, subcontractor, and closeout semantics specific to commercial construction.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Request that implies licensed trade or code-interpretation authority
Metric definition is ambiguous or disputed
Source data is incomplete, stale, or conflicting
Stakeholder asks for a claim or disclosure not supported by the data
Different systems disagree on the metric outcome
A stakeholder asks for stronger conclusions than the data supports

unresolved_ambiguities.detail: Temporary occupancy, substantial completion, turnover, and final signoff labels vary by AHJ and owner. The package treats local vocabulary and precedence as tenant-supplied runtime context rather than guessing.
